parameters.description: Number of results per page (default is 25)

parameters.name: ordering
parameters.description: Field name to order by (use -prefix for descending)

parameters.name: received_after
parameters.description: Filter documents received after this date (yyyy-mm-dd)

parameters.name: received_before
parameters.description: Filter documents received before this date (yyyy-mm-dd)

parameters.name: tz
parameters.description: Timezone for date filters (e.g. Asia%2FSingapore). Defaults to UTC.

parameters.name: with_result
parameters.description: Include parsed result string with each document

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results per page (default is 25)

parameters.name: ordering
parameters.description: Field name to order by (use -prefix for descending)

parser_field_id.description: Parser field ID starting with PF

choice_set.description: List of possible choice values. Only used when format is ONELINE.

used_by_ai.description: If false, that field will not be extracted by AI engines

parser_field.description: Only for template fields of type FIELD

result.description: Only present when using with_result=true query parameter

account_uuid.description: The Universally Unique Identifier for your account.

ai_engine.description: The AI engine being used for this parser, if any.

ai_instructions.description: General AI instruction for this parser.

allowed_extensions.description: List of files extensions to process. If not present, all extensions will be enabled.

attachments_only.description: Whether the parser in question skips email processing or not (read-only).

can_transform.description: Whether Python Post-Processing is enabled for this parser.

disable_deskew.description: Whether deskew is disabled for this parser (enabled by default).

document_count.description: The number of documents in the parser.

INCOMING.description: Files that have been received by our system but are awaiting processing.

ANALYZING.description: Files being analyzed against our system's import parameters and the user's mailbox settings.

PROGRESS.description: Files currently being processed by active AI engine for that mailbox.

PARSEDOK.description: Files that have been processed and data is available for export.

QUOTAEXC.description: Files where processing was stopped because the user does not have enough credits to process them.

SKIPPED.description: Files that were skipped because of a template.

SPLIT.description: Files that were split into multiple documents.

EXPORTKO.description: Files where exporting the parsed data failed.

TRANSKO.description: Files where our Python post-processing failed to process.

INVALID.description: Files that were not processed because they are not supported by our system.

document_per_status_count.description: This is a breakdown of the documents in the parser by status.

email_prefix.description: The email prefix for the parser.

even_pages.description: Whether the parser is configured to process only even page numbers in a document.

force_ocr.description: Whether Parseur runs its own OCR on every document uploaded to this parser.

id.description: The internal ID number for the parser.

is_master.description: Whether this is a master parser that passes its instructions on to others.

master_parser_name.description: The name of this parser's master parser.

master_parser_slug.description: The slug of this parser's master parser.

odd_pages.description: Whether the parser is configured to process only odd page numbers in a document.

page_range_set.description: The range of pages set to be processed in the parser.

parser_object_count.description: The total number of field and metadata outputs currently configured in the parser.

process_attachments.description: Whether email attachment processing is enabled on this parser.

retention_policy.description: How many days documents and data are stored on our servers before deletion (90 days is the detaulf).

split_keywords.description: The keyword(s) the parser will look for when splitting documents by page.

split_page.description: If not 0, this is the number of pages the parser will use to split in a file uploaded to this parser. If set to three, the parser will split a 30 page document 10 times, resulting in 10 files.

split_page_range_set.description: The specific page ranges set to be captured in this parser. Can have multiple values, i.e. 1,3-5,(1)

template_count.description: The number of templates the parser has.

webhook_count.description: The number of webhooks the parser has.
